articles.title: Tewkesbury Mother Sentenced to 13 Years for Enslaving Woman for 25 Years
articles.summary: A mother-of-ten has been sentenced to 13 years in prison for enslaving a vulnerable woman for 25 years in Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire. The victim suffered physical and emotional abuse, including being squirted with washing-up liquid and being forced to live off scraps.

articles.title: G7 Supports Record Oil Reserve Release Amid Price Surge
articles.summary: G7 nations support the release of oil from their reserves to tackle soaring prices, with the International Energy Agency potentially releasing 300 to 400 million barrels of oil.

articles.title: Teenager Arrested After Girl Stabbed at Norfolk School
articles.summary: A 16-year-old boy has been arrested after a teenage girl was stabbed at Thorpe St Andrew School in Norfolk, with the students placed under lockdown conditions. The victim suffered minor injuries.

articles.title: Couple Dies After Being Pulled from Water at Australian Beach
articles.summary: A married couple from the UK died after being pulled from the water at Shellharbour beach in New South Wales, Australia. Police praised the efforts of members of the public who attempted to save the couple.

articles.title: Mortgage Rates Surge Amid Iran War Uncertainty
articles.summary: The UK mortgage market is experiencing its most intense turbulence since the mini-Budget of 2022, with mortgage rates rising and nearly 500 products being pulled from the market.

articles.title: Global Music Trends: English No Longer Dominant in Charts, Says Spotify
articles.summary: According to Spotify, pop music's bias towards English is fading, with songs in 16 different languages appearing in its Global Top 50 last year. The company also reported a 6% increase in royalties paid to UK artists.

articles.title: UK Government to Publish Mandelson Documents on Wednesday
articles.summary: The UK government will publish internal documents on Lord Mandelson's appointment as British ambassador to the US on Wednesday, following a parliamentary procedure to compel the release of the papers.

articles.title: Russian Courier Speaks Out on Exploding Parcels Plot Against UK
articles.summary: A Russian courier has spoken out on his involvement in a plot to send exploding parcels to the UK and other countries, alleging he was hired to deliver the parcels without knowing their true contents.

articles.title: Iranian Football Delegation Member Reconsiders Australian Asylum
articles.summary: One member of the Iranian football delegation who initially accepted asylum in Australia has changed her mind, while the remaining players have left the country.

articles.title: UK Government Bans Al Quds Day March Over Public Disorder Fears
articles.summary: The UK government has banned the annual Al Quds Day march in London due to concerns over public disorder, with a stationary demonstration to be held instead under strict conditions.

articles.title: UK Weather: Met Office Issues Yellow Wind Warning Amid Snow and Cold Return
articles.summary: The Met Office has issued a yellow wind warning for the UK, with strengthening winds developing across the country and temperatures set to drop with a return to wintry weather.

articles.title: Epstein's Aides Still Control His Money and Secrets, Despite Congressional Subpoena
articles.summary: Richard Kahn and Darren Indyke, the sole executors of Jeffrey Epstein's estate, are still controlling his wealth and possessions, despite being subpoenaed to testify before a congressional committee.

articles.title: Insurgents Shake Up Vets Market with Affordable Options
articles.summary: Pet owners in the UK are struggling to afford rising vet bills, which have increased by 63% between 2016 and 2023. The Competition and Markets Authority has launched an investigation into the industry to address the issue.

articles.title: Former Deputy PM Clegg Criticizes University Tuition Fee System
articles.summary: Former Deputy PM Sir Nick Clegg has criticized the university tuition fee system, describing it as "deeply unfair" due to the repeated freezing of graduate repayment thresholds.

articles.title: Last Hereditary Peers to Leave House of Lords
articles.summary: The House of Lords (Hereditary Peers) Bill has passed, paving the way for the removal of the last remaining 92 hereditary peers from the upper house.
